display.purpose: All headings (h1-h6), headword titles. Normal weight only.

body.purpose: Paragraph/list copy, buttons. 1px letter-spacing is deliberate.

label.purpose: Badges, stat labels, grammar tags. Uppercase, dense, functional.

shadows.name: paper-lift
shadows.purpose: The system's only shadow, applied uniformly to every raised surface (section-cards, word-hero).

motion.name: button-transition
motion.purpose: Default button state transition (background, border, color).

components.name: Default Button
components.description: Standard action button: flat, square corners, dashed hover border.
components.css: .ds-btn { cursor: pointer; padding: 0.25rem; background-color: #fcead7; border: 1px solid #b23b27; color: #2b333b; font-family: 'Berlin', sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px; transition: all 0.3s ease-in-out; } .ds-btn:hover, .ds-btn:focus-visible { border-style: dashed; box-shadow: 0 0 12px 0 color-mix(in srgb, #cf5736 60%, white); }

components.name: Themed Button
components.description: Declarative primary-action button on ink-base fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-theme { cursor: pointer; padding: 0.25rem; background-color: #2b333b; border: 1px solid #b23b27; color: #f9f9f9; font-family: 'Berliner', serif; transition: all 0.3s ease-in-out; } .ds-btn-theme:hover, .ds-btn-theme:focus-visible { border-style: dashed; box-shadow: 0 0 12px 0 color-mix(in srgb, #cf5736 60%, white); }

components.name: Section Card
components.description: Numbered content card with dashed dividers and Paper Lift shadow.
components.html:
01

Etymologie

Herkunft und Bedeutung des Begriffs.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px dashed #4e545a; border-radius: 4px 4px 0 0; box-shadow: 0 0.5px 0.6px rgb(0 0 0 / 8%), 0 4px 5px rgb(0 0 0 / 16%); } .ds-card__header { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 0.625rem; padding: 0.75rem 1.25rem; border-bottom: 1px dashed #fad0b0; } .ds-card__num { font-size: 0.65rem; font-weight: 700; color: #cf5736; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-card__title { font-family: 'Berlin', sans-serif; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700; color: #2b333b; margin: 0; } .ds-card__body { padding: 1rem 1.25rem; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Uppercase metadata chip (word-of-day, word type, grammar tag).
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 0.25rem 0.6rem; border-radius: 4px; background: color-mix(in srgb, #fad0b0 60%, white); border: 1px solid #f0ac85; color: #2b333b; }

narrative.overview: Every raised surface in this system carries the same texture: a dashed hand-stamped line. It shows up as the underline beneath a headword, the divider between a section-card's header and body, the hover border on a button, the underline on an inline link. That one repeated stroke is doing the work most design systems spend a whole 'personality' section trying to fake — it reads as annotated, postcard-stamped, a little scrappy. Cheeky (schnodderig) and warm (heimatlich) at once, the way the Berlin dialect itself is blunt but affectionate. The palette sits a dark navy-ink base (#2b333b) against a Currywurst-orange family that carries almost every accent, badge, border, and stat highlight, with Berlin Red reserved as the rarer, more declarative color. This is a product register: the job is fast lookup, so decoration never gets between a search and its answer.
keyCharacteristics: Dashed-line texture as the one recurring signature (borders, underlines, dividers)
Currywurst orange dominant; Berlin Red rare and declarative
Dark navy-ink (#2b333b) carries text and dark-mode surfaces, never a bright accent
Flat, square-cornered buttons (0 radius); soft 4px radius everywhere else
One consistent Paper Lift shadow for every raised card/hero, no ad hoc shadows

rules.name: The Ink Stays Structural Rule
rules.body: The navy-ink base (#2b333b) is text and dark-mode surface only. If it starts appearing as a button fill or badge background in light mode, re-route to orange or red.

rules.name: The One Red Rule
rules.body: Berlin Red is the brand's own color, not a general accent. It shows up rarely so that when it appears, it still reads as THE color.

rules.name: The Handwritten Headline Rule
rules.body: Headings are always Berliner; body is always Berlin. The two fonts never trade places.

rules.name: The One Shadow Rule
rules.body: A new 'raised' component uses Paper Lift exactly as specified, not a lighter or heavier variant.

dos: Do use the dashed-line texture (borders, underlines, dividers) as the system's one recurring signature.
Do keep Currywurst Orange as the dominant accent family; reserve Berlin Red for rare, declarative brand moments only.
Do apply Paper Lift as the only shadow for raised surfaces.
Do set headings in Berliner and body copy in Berlin, with the 1px body letter-spacing intact.
Do keep functional metadata uppercase and small, never a decorative section eyebrow.
Do hold WCAG AA contrast (>=4.5:1 body text) even where the palette leans warm/orange on cream.
donts: Don't flatten the Schnauze voice into generic SaaS/dictionary-app neutrality.
Don't add new colored border-left/border-inline-start accent stripes beyond the one named exception (infotext citation callout).
Don't use the ink-base navy (#2b333b) as a bright UI accent.
Don't invent a new shadow value for a raised component — reuse Paper Lift exactly.
Don't round button corners — flat/square is the button shape.
Don't swap Berliner and Berlin between roles.
